event: younger sister died from metastatic myxoid liposarcoma
radiological examination showed nodular area of about 30 mm
pathological examination confirmed invasive ductal carcinoma, G3, with triple-negative phenotype and MIB1 85%
staging CT scan negative for distant metastasis
neoadjuvant chemotherapy with paclitaxel and carboplatin planned
skin overlying PORT showed signs of subcutaneous cellulitis
broad-spectrum intravenous antibiotic therapy with piperacillin/tazobactam and daptomycin started
peripheral blood cultures negative for infection
catheter tip positive for Klebsiella pneumoniae
antibiotic therapy modified to meropenem and levofloxacin
left thoracoscopy with pleural and mediastinal drainage
intensive inflammatory infiltrate including mainly neutrophils
chest X-ray showed progressive resolution of mediastinitis and pleural effusion
CT scan showed progressive resolution of mediastinitis and pleural effusion
wound improvement with scar was evident
blood works indicated progressive normalization of blood count and flogosis index
breast ultrasound showed no change in the dimension of the lump
breast surgical wound healing was regular
pathology assessment revealed fibroelastosis and chronic inflammation
isolated neoplastic cells for maximum 2 mm extension
restaging brain/chest/abdomen CT negative for distant metastasis
resumed chemotherapy with carboplatin and paclitaxel with dose reduction
